summary: Implement a nightly Azure Function (1985-NY-Yankees) that queries GPT-4o via TRAPI to retrieve the roster of the 1985 New York Yankees and persists the results to Azure Blob Storage. The function runs on a timer trigger (nightly schedule), uses Managed Identity for all auth (no API keys), and is deployed to Azure via IaC.
assumptions: TRAPI endpoint for GPT-4o is accessible via Managed Identity / OAuth2 token and does not require a static API key.
The programming language will be Python (common for Azure Functions + OpenAI integrations).
Azure Blob Storage will use a single container; one JSON blob per execution (date-stamped).
Infrastructure as Code will use Bicep, consistent with existing repo patterns under /infra.
The Azure Function will run on a Consumption or Flex Consumption plan.
Application Insights will be used for observability.
The nightly timer cron expression will be '0 0 2 * * *' (2 AM UTC daily).
TRAPI refers to the internal proxy/gateway for Azure OpenAI GPT-4o and supports Managed Identity.
The spec does not define auto-approval; manual approval of the task plan is required.
open_questions: What is the exact TRAPI endpoint URL and authentication flow (Managed Identity scope, OAuth audience)?
What language/runtime is preferred for the Azure Function (Python 3.11 assumed)?
Should each nightly run append to an existing blob or write a new dated blob?
What is the target Azure subscription, resource group, and region?
Is there a desired nightly execution time / timezone besides the assumed 2 AM UTC?
What Azure Function hosting plan (Consumption, Flex Consumption, Premium) is preferred?
Are there data retention or blob lifecycle policies required for stored player data?
What GitHub Actions secrets/OIDC federation setup is already in place for Azure deployments?

waves.name: Foundation
waves.objective: Approve the task plan, provision core Azure infrastructure (Function App, Storage, Managed Identity, App Insights), and establish CI/CD pipeline scaffolding.
entry_criteria: spec.md reviewed and accepted by owner (rciapala)
Target Azure subscription and resource group confirmed
TRAPI endpoint URL and auth scope confirmed
exit_criteria: Task plan approved and GitHub issues created
Azure infrastructure deployed via Bicep: Function App, Storage Account, Managed Identity, App Insights
GitHub Actions pipeline runs and deploys a hello-world Function successfully
Managed Identity has Storage Blob Data Contributor and TRAPI access roles assigned

waves.name: Core Build
waves.objective: Implement the three functional requirements: TRAPI/GPT-4o player retrieval, Blob Storage persistence, and nightly timer trigger wiring.
entry_criteria: TRAPI endpoint and auth confirmed working with Managed Identity
exit_criteria: Azure Function retrieves 1985 Yankees roster from GPT-4o via TRAPI
Retrieved roster is written to Azure Blob Storage as a dated JSON blob
Timer trigger fires on nightly cron schedule
All unit tests pass with >80% coverage

waves.name: Hardening
waves.objective: Add reliability, error handling, security controls, and observability to make the solution production-ready.
entry_criteria: Core Build wave exit criteria met
Function deploys and runs end-to-end in non-production environment
exit_criteria: Retry logic and poison-message handling implemented
Structured logging and Application Insights telemetry verified
Alerts configured for function failures and missed executions
Security review completed: no secrets in code, Managed Identity confirmed, least-privilege RBAC verified

waves.name: Release Readiness
waves.objective: Validate end-to-end in production, confirm nightly execution, and complete operational documentation.
exit_criteria: Function deployed to production and nightly run confirmed successful
Blob Storage contains valid 1985 Yankees roster output
Monitoring dashboard active with no open critical alerts

tasks.title: Review and approve implementation task plan
tasks.description: Review this engineering task plan for completeness, correctness, and alignment with spec.md. Confirm assumptions, resolve open questions, and approve to unblock downstream tasks.
outputs: GitHub issues created for all tasks
acceptance_criteria: Task plan reviewed by owner (rciapala) and tech lead
All open questions assigned owners or answered
GitHub issues created for all tasks with correct labels and assignees
Plan approved and Foundation wave tasks unblocked
risks: Unresolved TRAPI auth details could block INF-02 and APP-01
Missing Azure subscription/region confirmation delays INF-01

tasks.title: Provision Azure infrastructure via Bicep (Function App, Storage, Identity, Monitoring)
tasks.description: Author and deploy Bicep templates to provision: Azure Function App (Consumption plan, Python 3.11), Azure Storage Account (Blob container for player data), User-Assigned Managed Identity, and Application Insights. Follow existing /infra patterns with project/owner tags.
outputs: Azure Storage Account with blob container
acceptance_criteria: Bicep templates deploy without errors via `az deployment group create`
Azure Function App is running and reachable
Storage Account created with a 'players' blob container
User-Assigned Managed Identity created and assigned to Function App
Application Insights connected to Function App
All resources tagged with project=1985-NY-Yankees and owner=rciapala
Deployment validated with `az resource list` showing all expected resources
PR created with @copilot assigned for review
risks: Region quota limits for Consumption plan
Bicep template drift from existing /infra patterns

tasks.title: Assign RBAC roles to Managed Identity (Storage Blob Data Contributor, TRAPI access)
tasks.description: Assign 'Storage Blob Data Contributor' role to the Managed Identity on the Storage Account, and configure TRAPI access for GPT-4o (scope/role assignment as required by TRAPI documentation). No static API keys or secrets are to be used.
inputs: INF-01 outputs (Managed Identity principal ID, Storage Account resource ID)
outputs: RBAC role assignment for Storage Blob Data Contributor
TRAPI access role/scope configured for Managed Identity
acceptance_criteria: Managed Identity has 'Storage Blob Data Contributor' role on the Storage Account (verified via `az role assignment list`)
Managed Identity can acquire a token for the TRAPI scope without errors
No static API keys or connection strings stored in Function App settings or code
Role assignments defined in Bicep templates (not manual portal assignments)
PR created with @copilot assigned for review
risks: TRAPI may require a specific Entra app registration or custom role not yet defined
Insufficient permissions to assign roles in target subscription

tasks.title: Scaffold GitHub Actions CI/CD pipeline for Azure Function deployment
tasks.description: Create a GitHub Actions workflow that lints, tests, builds, and deploys the Azure Function to Azure using OIDC federated identity (no stored credentials). Include separate jobs for PR validation and deployment to production.
inputs: Azure Function App name and resource group from INF-01
GitHub OIDC federation setup for Azure
outputs: GitHub OIDC federated credential configured in Azure AD
Successful deployment run in GitHub Actions
acceptance_criteria: GitHub Actions workflow triggers on push to main and on pull requests
PR validation job runs linting and unit tests and must pass before merge
Deployment job deploys Function App using OIDC (no stored secrets in GitHub)
A hello-world function deploys successfully via the pipeline
Workflow run link provided as evidence of successful deployment
PR created with @copilot assigned for review
risks: OIDC federation setup may require Entra app registration permissions
Existing GitHub Actions secrets setup may conflict or be absent

tasks.title: Implement TRAPI/GPT-4o integration to retrieve 1985 Yankees roster
tasks.description: Implement a Python module that authenticates to TRAPI using the Function App's Managed Identity (azure-identity DefaultAzureCredential), calls GPT-4o with a prompt to list 1985 New York Yankees members, and returns a structured list of players.
inputs: TRAPI endpoint URL and OAuth scope (from PRJ-01/INF-02)
azure-identity and openai Python SDK docs
acceptance_criteria: Module authenticates to TRAPI using DefaultAzureCredential (no hardcoded API keys)
GPT-4o prompt returns a non-empty list of 1985 Yankees players
Returned data is a structured list (JSON-serializable)
Unit tests mock TRAPI calls and assert correct parsing of GPT-4o responses
All unit tests pass (`pytest tests/test_trapi_client.py` with link to test results)
Code coverage >= 80% for this module
PR created with @copilot assigned for review
risks: GPT-4o response format may be non-deterministic; prompt engineering required
TRAPI auth scope unknown until PRJ-01 open questions resolved
Token acquisition may fail in local dev without Managed Identity context

tasks.title: Implement Azure Blob Storage writer for player roster
tasks.description: Implement a Python module that uses the azure-storage-blob SDK with DefaultAzureCredential to write the retrieved player roster as a dated JSON blob (e.g., players-YYYY-MM-DD.json) to the 'players' container in Azure Blob Storage.
inputs: Storage Account name and container name from INF-01
INF-02 RBAC assignment for Storage Blob Data Contributor
acceptance_criteria: Module writes JSON blob to 'players' container using DefaultAzureCredential (no connection strings)
Blob is named with date stamp (players-YYYY-MM-DD.json)
Unit tests mock azure-storage-blob calls and assert correct blob name and content
All unit tests pass (`pytest tests/test_blob_writer.py` with link to test results)
Code coverage >= 80% for this module
PR created with @copilot assigned for review
risks: Managed Identity may not have Storage Blob Data Contributor in local dev; use Azurite or environment variable override for testing

tasks.title: Implement nightly timer-triggered Azure Function orchestrating retrieval and storage
tasks.description: Implement the main Azure Function with a Timer trigger (cron: '0 0 2 * * *' for 2 AM UTC nightly) that calls the TRAPI client (APP-01) and Blob writer (APP-02) in sequence, with structured logging for each step.
inputs: Azure Functions Python worker docs for Timer trigger
outputs: Unit and integration tests in tests/test_function_app.py
acceptance_criteria: Function is decorated with @app.timer_trigger with cron schedule '0 0 2 * * *'
Function calls trapi_client and blob_writer in correct sequence
Structured log messages emitted at start, after retrieval, after storage, and on error
Unit tests mock trapi_client and blob_writer and assert orchestration logic
All unit tests pass (`pytest tests/test_function_app.py` with link to test results)
Function deploys via CI/CD pipeline (REL-01) without errors
PR created with @copilot assigned for review
risks: Timer trigger cron syntax differs between Azure Functions v1/v2; confirm correct format for Python v2 programming model
Cold start latency on Consumption plan may affect first nightly run

tasks.title: Security review: validate no secrets in code, Managed Identity end-to-end, least-privilege RBAC
tasks.description: Conduct a security review of the full codebase and Bicep templates: verify no API keys or secrets in source, confirm all Azure resource access uses Managed Identity, and validate RBAC assignments follow least-privilege principles.
outputs: GitHub secret scanning enabled on repository
acceptance_criteria: GitHub secret scanning shows no detected secrets in repository
No hardcoded credentials, connection strings, or API keys in any source file or workflow
All Azure SDK calls use DefaultAzureCredential
RBAC assignments are least-privilege (no Owner or Contributor assigned where data-plane roles suffice)
Findings documented and all critical/high findings remediated before release
PR created with @copilot assigned for review
risks: Developer convenience workarounds (local .env files) may accidentally be committed
TRAPI access scope may grant broader permissions than intended

tasks.title: Implement retry logic and error handling in Function execution
tasks.description: Add retry logic (with exponential backoff) to TRAPI calls and Blob Storage writes, and implement structured error handling so transient failures are retried and persistent failures are logged with full context before the function exits with a non-zero status.
outputs: Updated src/trapi_client.py and src/blob_writer.py with retry logic
acceptance_criteria: TRAPI client retries up to 3 times with exponential backoff on transient errors (5xx, timeout)
Blob writer retries up to 3 times on transient storage errors
Persistent failures log error with full exception context and re-raise
Unit tests simulate transient failures and assert retry behavior
All unit tests pass with link to test results
PR created with @copilot assigned for review
risks: Overly aggressive retries may cause nightly run to exceed Function timeout (10 min on Consumption)

tasks.title: Configure Application Insights structured logging, metrics, and nightly execution alerts
tasks.description: Configure structured logging (JSON format) in the Azure Function with Application Insights integration. Create alert rules for: function execution failures, and missed nightly executions (no successful run in 25 hours).
outputs: Structured logging integrated via opencensus or azure-monitor-opentelemetry
Alert rules deployed via Bicep in /infra
acceptance_criteria: Function logs appear in Application Insights with structured fields (run_date, player_count, duration_ms, status)
Alert rule fires when function execution fails (tested by intentional failure in non-prod)
Alert rule fires when no successful execution in 25 hours
Alerts configured with notification channel (email or Teams webhook)
Alert rules defined in Bicep and deployed via CI/CD
PR created with @copilot assigned for review
risks: Application Insights sampling may drop logs if misconfigured
Alert for missed execution requires custom metric or Log Analytics query

tasks.title: End-to-end integration test: nightly function run validates roster in Blob Storage
tasks.description: Write an integration test that manually triggers the Azure Function (or invokes it via HTTP admin endpoint) in a non-production environment, then asserts that a valid dated JSON blob containing 1985 Yankees players exists in the Storage Account.
inputs: Deployed non-prod Function App (from REL-01 + APP-03)
List of expected 1985 Yankees players for assertion
outputs: Test execution results and evidence blob in Storage
acceptance_criteria: Integration test triggers function and waits for completion
Test asserts blob 'players-YYYY-MM-DD.json' exists in 'players' container
Test asserts blob content is valid JSON with a non-empty list of player names
Test asserts player count is plausible (>= 25 roster members)
Test results published as GitHub Actions artifact with link
PR created with @copilot assigned for review
risks: GPT-4o responses may vary; assertion on exact player list may be brittle — use minimum count threshold
Integration test requires deployed non-prod environment with correct RBAC

tasks.title: Author operational runbook and README for 1985-NY-Yankees function
tasks.description: Write a README.md covering: architecture overview, deployment steps, nightly schedule, how to manually trigger the function, how to read player blobs from Storage, and a runbook section for common failure scenarios (TRAPI timeout, storage failure, missed execution).
inputs: All completed app and infra tasks
outputs: RUNBOOK.md or runbook section within README
acceptance_criteria: README covers architecture, deployment, schedule, and manual trigger steps
Runbook covers TRAPI failure, Storage failure, and missed nightly execution scenarios
Deployment steps are validated end-to-end by a team member following the README
PR created with @copilot assigned for review
risks: Documentation becomes stale if written before final implementation details are confirmed

tasks.title: Deploy to production and validate first nightly execution
tasks.description: Execute production deployment via CI/CD pipeline, confirm nightly timer trigger fires at scheduled time, and validate that a valid player roster blob is written to the production Storage Account.
inputs: Production Azure subscription and resource group
outputs: Production Function App running on nightly schedule
acceptance_criteria: Production deployment completes via GitHub Actions with no errors

Nightly timer trigger fires at scheduled time (verified in Application Insights logs)
Valid players-YYYY-MM-DD.json blob exists in production 'players' container

Release sign-off by rciapala documented in GitHub PR or issue
